" <<The information specialist>> <<models the problem>> <<using graph theory>> <<and linear algebra>>. <<The adjacency matrix>> <<captures citation data>> <<as weighted edges>> <<between nodes>>. <<The matrix exponential>> <<is defined for>> <<a continuous-time>> <<random walk>>.

* * *

<<The element (e^A)_{ij}>> <<represents expected>> <<times vertex j>> <<is reached from i>>. <<In continuous-time>> <<random walks transitions>> <<occur at rates>> <<given by weights>>. <<This model helps>> <<predict access patterns>> <<for efficient retrieval>> <<and storage>>.

* * *

<<To reduce storage>> <<the specialist uses>> <<SVD for rank reduction>> <<of matrix A>>. <<The optimal B_k>> <<is formed from>> <<top k singular values>> <<and vectors>>. <<This minimizes>> <<Frobenius norm error>> <<while preserving>> <<key structural data>>."
